title: rhamnus smithii subsp. typica
intro: Rhamnus smithii subsp. typica, also known as the Smith's buckthorn, is a plant species native to New Zealand, belonging to the Rhamnaceae family. This plant has been found to have medicinal properties, particularly its bark, which has been used to treat various health conditions due to its anthraquinone content. The health benefits of Smith's buckthorn include its potential to aid in digestion, reduce inflammation, and exhibit antimicrobial properties. The plant is typically prepared as an herbal decoction, infusion, or tincture, which can be used to treat gastrointestinal issues, such as constipation and diarrhea.
